channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Iran Hacktivists Make Noise but Have Little Impact on War
item.description: Iran-aligned groups are trying to make their mark in the Gulf, but the results have fallen short of remarkable.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Checkmarx KICS Code Scanner Targeted in Widening Supply Chain Hit
item.description: TeamPCP is the likely cyber threat actor behind attacks on Trivy, Checkmarx's KICS and VS Code plug-ins, and the LiteLLM AI library — and all signs point to more attacks to come.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: How AI Coding Tools Crushed the Endpoint Security Fortress
item.description: Security vendors have spent years building up defenses around the endpoint, but one researcher says AI coding tools have brought the walls down.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: RSA Conference: UK NCSC Head Urges Industry to Develop Vibe Coding Safeguards
item.description: The head of the UK’s NCSC is calling the cybersecurity industry to “seize the disruptive vibe coding opportunity” to make software more secure

item.title: TeamPCP Backdoors LiteLLM Versions 1.82.7–1.82.8 via Trivy CI/CD Compromise
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recent compromises of Trivy and KICS, has now compromised a popular Python package named litellm, pushing two malicious versions containing a credential harvester, a Kubernetes lateral movement toolkit, and a persistent backdoor. Multiple security vendors, including Endor Labs and JFrog, revealed that litellm versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 were published on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Tax Search Ads Deliver ScreenConnect Malware Using Huawei Driver to Disable EDR
item.description: A large-scale malvertising campaign active since January 2026 has been observed targeting U.S.-based individuals searching for tax-related documents to serve rogue installers for ConnectWise ScreenConnect that drop a tool named HwAudKiller to blind security programs using the bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) technique. "The campaign abuses Google Ads to serve rogue ScreenConnect (
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 5 Learnings from the First-Ever Gartner Market Guide for Guardian Agents
item.description: On February 25, 2026, Gartner published its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, marking an important milestone for this emerging category. For those unfamiliar with the various Gartner report types, “a Market Guide defines a market and explains what clients can expect it to do in the short term. With the focus on early, more chaotic markets, a Market Guide does not rate or position
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Use Fake Resumes to Steal Enterprise Credentials and Deploy Crypto Miner
item.description: An ongoing phishing campaign is targeting French-speaking corporate environments with fake resumes that lead to the deployment of cryptocurrency miners and information stealers. "The campaign uses highly obfuscated VBScript files disguised as resume/CV documents, delivered through phishing emails," Securonix researchers Shikha Sangwan, Akshay Gaikwad, and Aaron Beardslee said in a report shared
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New ‘DarkSword’ Leak Puts Millions of iPhones at Risk After Initial Attack
item.description: <p>A newer DarkSword exploit leak makes hacking outdated iPhones easier, exposing hundreds of millions of devices to risk.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-darksword-leak-iphone-exploit-risk-after-initial-attack/">New ‘DarkSword’ Leak Puts Millions of iPhones at Risk After Initial Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Silver Fox Cyber Campaigns Show Shift Toward Dual Espionage
item.description: Silver Fox pivots from ValleyRAT tax lures to WhatsApp‑style stealers, blending espionage & phishing

item.title: Millions of Anonymous Student and Crime Tips Exposed in Major Data Breach
item.description: <p>A reported breach of P3 Global Intel exposed millions of anonymous crime and school safety tips, raising new concerns about privacy and trust.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-p3-global-intel-data-breach/">Millions of Anonymous Student and Crime Tips Exposed in Major Data Breach</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Citrix Urges Immediate Patching for Critical NetScaler Vulnerabilities
item.description: A critical vulnerability in Citrix’s NetScaler products allows unauthenticated remote attackers to leak information from the appliance's memory

item.title: GitHub 'OpenClaw Deployer' Repo Delivers Trojan Instead
item.description: An AI-assisted campaign is spreading more than 300 poisoned packages for diverse assets ranging from developer tools to game cheats.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: New Npm 'Ghost Campaign' Uses Fake Install Logs to Hide Malware
item.description: Ghost npm campaign fakes install logs to steal sudo passwords and drop RATs that loot crypto and data

item.title: Former Ukrainian Foreign Minister Dmytro Kuleba to Address the New Cyber Frontline at Infosecurity Europe
item.description: Geopolitics and cyber warfare take center stage at Infosecurity Europe as Dmytro Kuleba discusses Ukraine’s hybrid war experience

item.title: How a Large Bank Uses AI Digital Twins for Threat Hunting
item.description: JPMorgan Chase uses digital fingerprints and digital twins to spot online attackers and malicious behaviors while also reducing pesky false alerts.
item.dc:creator: Bree Fowler

item.title: Enterprise Cybersecurity Software Fails 20% of the Time, Warns Absolute Security
item.description: Poor patch management, increasingly complex IT environments and continued use of obsolete software puts organizations at risk from cyber threats, says the Absolute Security 2026 Resilience Risk Index

item.title: Microsoft Proposes Better Identity, Guardrails for AI Agents
item.description: Companies need better controls to manage key threats rising from the growth of agentic AI. These new features provide a starting point.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Cybersecurity Specialization: Losing Foundational Skills
item.description: Cybersecurity has changed fast. Roles are more&nbsp;specialized, and tooling is more advanced. On paper, this should make organizations more secure. But in practice, many teams struggle with the same basic problems they faced years ago: unclear risk priorities, misaligned tooling decisions, and difficulty explaining security issues in terms the business understands. These challenges do not
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ghost Campaign Uses 7 npm Packages to Steal Crypto Wallets and Credentials
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a new set of malicious npm packages that are designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets and sensitive data. The activity is being tracked by ReversingLabs as the Ghost campaign. The list of identified packages, all published by a user named mikilanjillo, is below -
react-performance-suite react-state-optimizer-core react-fast-utilsa ai-fast-auto-trader
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Team Mirai and Democracy
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: TeamPCP Hacks Checkmarx GitHub Actions Using Stolen CI Credentials
item.description:
Two more GitHub Actions workflows have become the latest to be compromised by credential-stealing malware by a threat actor known as TeamPCP, the cloud-native cybercriminal operation also behind the Trivy supply chain attack. The workflows, both maintained by the supply chain security company Checkmarx, are listed below -
checkmarx/ast-github-action checkmarx/kics-github-action
Cloud security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Russian Initial Access Broker Handed 81-Month Sentence
item.description: Russian cybercriminal Aleksei Volkov has received close to seven years behind bars for role in Yanluowang ransomware

item.title: Cloud workload security: Mind the gaps
item.description: As IT infrastructure expands, visibility and control often lag behind – until an incident forces a reckoning

item.title: Handala Group Tied to Iranian Hack‑and‑Leak Operations, FBI Reveals
item.description: The FBI has warned that Iranian hacking group Handala has been targeting opponents of the regime since 2023

item.title: U.S. Sentences Russian Hacker to 6.75 Years for Role in $9M Ransomware Damage
item.description: A 26-year-old Russian citizen has been sentenced in the U.S. to 6.75 years (81 months) in prison for his role in assisting major cybercrime groups, including the Yanluowang ransomware crew, in conducting numerous attacks against U.S. companies and other organizations. According to the U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ), Aleksei Olegovich Volkov facilitated dozens of ransomware attacks across the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Citrix Urges Patching Critical NetScaler Flaw Allowing Unauthenticated Data Leaks
item.description:
Citrix has released security updates to address two vulnerabilities in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway, including a critical flaw that could be exploited to leak sensitive data from the application. The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS score: 9.3) - Insufficient input validation leading to memory overread CVE-2026-4368 (CVSS score: 7.7) - Race condition leading to user
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI in the SOC: What Could Go Wrong?
item.description: Two cybersecurity leaders tested out AI in their respective SOCs for six months — and here's what they learned.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bracken

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Targets CI/CD Secrets
item.description: A threat actor used the open source security tool to deploy an infostealer into CI/CD workflows and steal cloud credentials, SSH keys, tokens, and other sensitive secrets.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Ransomware's New Era: Moving at AI Speed
item.description: Threat actors bypass security tools and use AI to launch faster ransomware attacks that exploit valid credentials and target data.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: CISOs Debate Human Role in AI-Powered Security
item.description: The idea of a &quot;human in the loop&quot; in AI deployment was challenged during a security executive panel at the RSAC 2026 Conference this week.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Navia Data Breach Hits 2.7 Million People, Exposing Sensitive Personal Data
item.description: <p>Navia Benefit Solutions says a data breach exposed personal and benefits data tied to 2.7 million people after weeks of unauthorized access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-navia-benefits-data-breach-2-7-million-exposed/">Navia Data Breach Hits 2.7 Million People, Exposing Sensitive Personal Data</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Google Reinvents Android Sideloading to Thwart Scammers
item.description: <p>Google is adding a stricter sideloading process on Android, preserving app installs from outside Google Play while making scam-driven abuse harder.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-android-sideloading-security-changes/">Google Reinvents Android Sideloading to Thwart Scammers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Madeline Clarke

item.title: North Korean Hackers Abuse VS Code Auto-Run Tasks to Deploy StoatWaffle Malware
item.description: The North Korean threat actors behind the Contagious Interview campaign, also tracked as WaterPlum, have been attributed to a malware family tracked as StoatWaffle that's distributed via malicious Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) projects. The use of VS Code "tasks.json" to distribute malware is a relatively new tactic adopted by the threat actor since December 2025, with the attacks
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most Cybersecurity Staff Don’t Know How Fast They Could Stop a Cyber-Attack on AI Systems
item.description: ISACA survey found that confusion over responsibility and lack of understanding around AI cyber-attacks makes containing them difficult

item.title: Tycoon2FA Phishing Service Resumes Activity Post-Takedown
item.description: Tycoon2FA phishing platform resumes activity post-takedown, leveraging AITM techniques to bypass MFA

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: High-Tech Sector Overtakes Finance as Top Target for Cyber-Attacks, Mandiant Reports
item.description: High tech was the most frequently targeted industry in Mandiant investigations in 2025, overtaking financial services which led in 2023 and 2024

item.title: Attackers Hide Infostealer in Copyright Infringement Notices
item.description: A phishing campaign targeting healthcare, government, hospitality, and education sectors in various countries uses several evasion techniques to avoid detection.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Expands With New Compromised Docker Images
item.description: New Trivy Docker images 0.69.5 and 0.69.6 compromised with TeamPCP infostealer, impacting CI/CD scans

item.title: Windows 11 Patch Triggers Sign-In Failures Across Microsoft Apps
item.description: <p>A Windows 11 security update triggered Microsoft app sign-in failures, prompting an emergency patch and a manual workaround for affected users.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-11-update-login-error-march-2026/">Windows 11 Patch Triggers Sign-In Failures Across Microsoft Apps</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: CI/CD Backdoor, FBI Buys Location Data, WhatsApp Ditches Numbers & More
item.description: Another week, another reminder that the internet is still a mess. Systems people thought were secure are being broken in simple ways, showing many still ignore basic advisories. This edition covers a mix of issues: supply chain attacks hitting CI/CD setups, long-abused IoT devices being shut down, and exploits moving quickly from disclosure to real attacks. There are also new malware tricks
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Reflections from the Second NIST Cyber AI Profile Workshop
item.description: Thank you to everyone who participated in the Cybersecurity Framework Profile for Artificial Intelligence (Cyber AI Profile) Workshop in January! The input we received on the Preliminary Draft during this workshop has been invaluable and is informing the development of the next draft of the NIST Cyber AI Profile. We are working toward publishing a full workshop summary soon that captures themes and highlights from the event. In the interim, we would like to share a preview of what we heard… Background on the Second Cyber AI Profile Workshop This workshop was a continuation of the past months
item.dc:creator: Katerina Megas, Barbara Cuthill, Julie Nethery Snyder , Christina Sames, Ishika Khemani

item.title: We Found Eight Attack Vectors Inside AWS Bedrock. Here's What Attackers Can Do with Them
item.description: AWS Bedrock is Amazon's platform for building AI-powered applications. It gives developers access to foundation models and the tools to connect those models directly to enterprise data and systems. That connectivity is what makes it powerful – but it’s also what makes Bedrock a target. When an AI agent can query your Salesforce instance, trigger a Lambda function, or pull from a SharePoint
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Xbox One Hacked
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Microsoft Warns IRS Phishing Hits 29,000 Users, Deploys RMM Malware
item.description: Microsoft has warned of fresh campaigns that are capitalizing on the upcoming tax season in the U.S. to harvest credentials and deliver malware. The email campaigns take advantage of the urgency and time-sensitive nature of emails to send phishing messages masquerading as refund notices, payroll forms, filing reminders, and requests from tax professionals to deceive recipients into opening
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Orders US Government to Patch Maximum Severity Cisco Flaw
item.description: CISA added CVE-2026-20131 to its KEV catalog as it is being used in ransomware campaigns

item.title: Operation Alice Takes Down 370,000+ Dark Web Sites
item.description: German-led policing effort against fraud operation disrupts countless CSAM and cybercrime sites

item.title: Trivy Hack Spreads Infostealer via Docker, Triggers Worm and Kubernetes Wiper
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered malicious artifacts distributed via Docker Hub following the Trivy supply chain attack, highlighting the widening blast radius across developer environments. The last known clean release of Trivy on Docker Hub is 0.69.3. The malicious versions 0.69.4, 0.69.5, and 0.69.6 have since been removed from the container image library. "New image tags 0.69.5 and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Proofpoint Redefines Email and Data Security for the Agentic Workspace
Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-32975 (CVSS 10.0) to Hijack Unpatched Quest KACE SMA Systems
item.description: Threat actors are suspected to be exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance (SMA), according to Arctic Wolf. The cybersecurity company said it observed malicious activity starting the week of March 9, 2026, in customer environments that's consistent with the exploitation of CVE-2025-32975 on unpatched SMA systems exposed to the internet. It's
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Dominates RSAC Innovation Sandbox
item.description: Ten finalists will each have three minutes to make their case for being the most innovative, promising young security company of the year.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Hackers Target Signal, WhatsApp in Mass Phishing Attacks
item.description: Threat actors affiliated with Russian Intelligence Services are conducting phishing campaigns to compromise commercial messaging applications (CMAs) like WhatsApp and Signal to seize control of accounts belonging to individuals with high intelligence value, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) and Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said Friday. "The campaign
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Oracle Patches Critical CVE-2026-21992 Enabling Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
item.description: Oracle has released security updates to address a critical security flaw impacting Identity Manager and Web Services Manager that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-21992, carries a CVSS score of 9.8 out of a maximum of 10.0. "This vulnerability is remotely exploitable without authentication," Oracle said in an advisory. "If successfully
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Triggers Self-Spreading CanisterWorm Across 47 npm Packages
item.description: The threat actors behind the supply chain attack targeting the popular Trivy scanner are suspected to be conducting follow-on attacks that have led to the compromise of a large number of npm packages with a previously undocumented self-propagating worm dubbed CanisterWorm. The name is a reference to the fact that the malware uses an ICP canister, which denotes a tamperproof smart contract on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Flags Apple, Craft CMS, Laravel Bugs in KEV, Orders Patching by April 3, 2026
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added five security flaws impacting Apple, Craft CMS, and Laravel Livewire to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, urging federal agencies to patch them by April 3, 2026. The vulnerabilities that have come under exploitation are listed below -
CVE-2025-31277 (CVSS score: 8.8) - A vulnerability in Apple
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jumbo Flying Squid in the South Pacific
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Patch Now: Oracle's Fusion Middleware Has Critical RCE Flaw
item.description: Attackers can execute arbitrary code without authentication if Oracle's Identity or Web Services Managers are exposed to the Web.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Trivy Security Scanner GitHub Actions Breached, 75 Tags Hijacked to Steal CI/CD Secrets
item.description: Trivy, a popular open-source vulnerability scanner maintained by Aqua Security, was compromised a second time within the span of a month to deliver malware capable of stealing sensitive CI/CD secrets. The latest incident impacted GitHub Actions "aquasecurity/trivy-action" and "aquasecurity/setup-trivy," which are used to scan Docker container images for vulnerabilities and set up GitHub Actions
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake ‘Trusted Sender’ Labels Misused in New Apple Mail Phishing Scheme
item.description: <p>Fake “trusted sender” labels can mislead users, as scammers embed them into email bodies to disguise phishing emails and create a false sense of security.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-mail-trusted-sender-phishing-scam/">Fake ‘Trusted Sender’ Labels Misused in New Apple Mail Phishing Scheme</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
item.description: Files on a central cloud server used by the ransomware group highlight a systematic, aggressive attack on network backups as a key TTP.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Critical Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-33017 Triggers Attacks within 20 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A critical security flaw impacting Langflow has come under active exploitation within 20 hours of public disclosure, highlighting the speed at which threat actors weaponize newly published vulnerabilities. The security defect, tracked as CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS score: 9.3), is a case of missing authentication combined with code injection that could result in remote code execution. "The POST /api/v1
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Factories, Security Flaws, and Workforce Shifts Define This Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from March 16–20.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-factories-security-flaws-and-workforce-shifts-define-this-week-in-tech/">AI Factories, Security Flaws, and Workforce Shifts Define This Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Targets Cisco Enterprise Firewalls
item.description: The ransomware gang, known for double-extortion attacks, had access to a critical Cisco firewall vulnerability weeks before it was publicly disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: All aboard: the NIST Cybersecurity for IoT Program is headed to our next stop! Share your input on where we’re headed during our Future Directions Two-Day Workshop on March 31st.
item.description: Workshop Details… We’re looking forward to hearing from the community during our “Future Directions” Workshop! Date: March 31 - April 1, 2026 Where: NIST’s Gaithersburg campus! Registration and Details: HERE Can’t make it? We still want to hear from you – email us at <span class="spamspan"><span class="u">IoTSecurity</span> [at] <span class="d">nist.gov</span><span class="t"> (IoTSecurity[at]nist[dot]gov)</span></span>. All Aboard for Product Cybersecurity The NIST Cybersecurity for Internet of Things (IoT) Program was established to help real-world practitioners navigate the gray areas between IT and connected products. This provides clarity when it comes to challenges, available existing resources, and understanding where
item.dc:creator: Michael Fagan

item.title: Proton Mail Shared User Information with the Police
item.description: <p>404 Media has a <a href="https://www.404media.co/proton-mail-helped-fbi-unmask-anonymous-stop-cop-city-protestor/">story</a> about Proton Mail giving subscriber data to the Swiss government, who passed the information to the FBI.</p> <p>It&#8217;s metadata&#8212;payment information related to a particular account&#8212;but still important knowledge. This sort of thing happens, even to privacy-centric companies like Proton Mail.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Google Adds 24-Hour Wait for Unverified App Sideloading to Reduce Malware and Scams
item.description: Google on Thursday announced a new "advanced flow" for Android sideloading that requires a mandatory 24-hour wait period to install apps from unverified developers in an attempt to balance openness with safety. The new changes come against the backdrop of a developer verification mandate the tech giant announced last year that requires all Android apps to be registered by verified developers to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Langflow Bug in Just 20 Hours
item.description: Sysdig details how threat actors exploited a critical CVE in Langflow in less than a day

item.title: The Importance of Behavioral Analytics in AI-Enabled Cyber Attacks
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is changing how individuals and organizations conduct many activities, including how cybercriminals carry out phishing attacks and iterate on malware. Now, cybercriminals are using AI to generate personalized phishing emails, deepfakes and malware that evade traditional detection by impersonating normal user activity and bypassing legacy security models. As a result,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Move fast and save things: A quick guide to recovering a hacked account
item.description: What you do – and how fast – after an account is compromised often matters more than it may seem

item.title: NCA Boss Warns That Teens Are Being “Radicalized” Into Cybercrime Online
item.description: The National Crime Agency’s director general warns that technology is rapidly reshaping crime

item.title: Magento PolyShell Flaw Enables Unauthenticated Uploads, RCE and Account Takeover
item.description: Sansec is warning of a critical security flaw in Magento's REST API that could allow unauthenticated attackers to upload arbitrary executables and achieve code execution and account takeover. The vulnerability has been codenamed PolyShell by Sansec owing to the fact that the attack hinges on disguising malicious code as an image. There is no evidence that the shortcoming has been exploited in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DoJ Disrupts 3 Million-Device IoT Botnets Behind Record 31.4 Tbps Global DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the disruption of command-and-control (C2) infrastructure used by several Internet of Things (IoT) botnets like AISURU, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad as part of a court-authorized law enforcement operation. The effort also saw authorities from Canada and Germany targeting the operators behind these botnets, with a number of private
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple Warns Older iPhones Vulnerable to Coruna, DarkSword Exploit Kit Attacks
item.description: Apple is urging users who are still running an outdated version of iOS to update their iPhones to secure against web-based attacks carried out via powerful exploit kits like Coruna and DarkSword. These attacks employ malicious web content to target out-of-date versions of iOS, triggering an infection chain that leads to the theft of sensitive data. "For example, if you're using an older
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: AI Conundrum: Why MCP Security Can't Be Patched Away
item.description: RSAC Conference Preview: MCP introduces security risks into LLM environments that are architectural and not easily fixable, researcher says.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: With Government's Role Uncertain, Businesses Unite to Combat Fraud
item.description: Major industry leaders agree to share information and collaborate to boost defenses in the wake of distressing online scams.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Native Launches With Security Control Plane for Multicloud
item.description: The cloud security startup's platform translates and enforces security policies across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle using provider-native controls.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: Speagle Malware Hijacks Cobra DocGuard to Steal Data via Compromised Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware dubbed Speagle that hijacks the functionality and infrastructure of a legitimate program called Cobra DocGuard. "Speagle is designed to surreptitiously harvest sensitive information from infected computers and transmit it to a Cobra DocGuard server that has been compromised by the attackers, masking the data exfiltration process as legitimate
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 35 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
item.description: A new analysis of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers has revealed that 54 of them leverage a technique known as bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) by abusing a total of 35 vulnerable drivers. EDR killer programs have been a common presence in ransomware intrusions as they offer a way for affiliates to neutralize security software before deploying file-encrypting malware. This
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Post-Quantum Web Could be Safer, Faster
item.description: Major providers are testing a quantum-safe version of HTTPS that shrinks certificates to one-tenth their previous size, decreasing latency and adding transparency.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Ransomware Affiliate Exposes Details of 'The Gentlemen' Operation
item.description: Hastalamuerte leaks The Gentlemen RaaS ops: FortiGate exploits, BYOVD evasion, Qilin split tactics

item.title: Financial Brands Targeted in Global Mobile Banking Malware Surge
item.description: Mobile banking malware targets over 1200 financial apps globally, shifting fraud to user devices

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: FortiGate RaaS, Citrix Exploits, MCP Abuse, LiveChat Phish & More
item.description: ThreatsDay Bulletin is back on The Hacker News, and this week feels off in a familiar way. Nothing loud, nothing breaking everything at once. Just a lot of small things that shouldn&rsquo;t work anymore but still do. Some of it looks simple, almost sloppy, until you see how well it lands. Other bits feel a little too practical, like they&rsquo;re already closer to real-world use than anyone
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Apple Hack: Up to 270M iPhones Vulnerable to ‘DarkSword’ Exploit
item.description: <p>Researchers uncover “DarkSword,” a powerful iPhone exploit targeting millions via compromised websites. Learn how it works and how to protect your device.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-darksword-iphone-exploit-ios-vulnerability/">New Apple Hack: Up to 270M iPhones Vulnerable to ‘DarkSword’ Exploit</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: New Perseus Android Banking Malware Monitors Notes Apps to Extract Sensitive Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Android malware family called Perseus that's being actively distributed in the wild with an aim to conduct device takeover (DTO) and financial fraud. Perseus is built upon the foundations of Cerberus and Phoenix, at the same time evolving into a "more flexible and capable platform" for compromising Android devices through dropper apps distributed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How Ceros Gives Security Teams Visibility and Control in Claude Code
item.description: Security teams have spent years building identity and access controls for human users and service accounts. But a new category of actor has quietly entered most enterprise environments, and it operates entirely outside those controls. Claude Code, Anthropic's AI coding agent, is now running across engineering organizations at scale. It reads files, executes shell commands, calls external APIs,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FCA Updates Cyber Incident and Third-Party Reporting Rules
item.description: The UK’s financial regulator has issued new rules to make incident and third-party reporting clearer

item.title: EDR killers explained: Beyond the drivers
item.description: ESET researchers dive deeper into the EDR killer ecosystem, disclosing how attackers abuse vulnerable drivers

item.title: AWS Warns Hackers Have Abused Cisco Firewall Zero-Day Since January
item.description: Notorious ransomware group Interlock has been exploiting a Cisco zero-day bug since January, AWS says

item.title: Hacking a Robot Vacuum
item.description: <p>Someone tries to remote control his own DJI Romo vacuum, and ends up controlling <a href="https://www.theverge.com/tech/879088/dji-romo-hack-vulnerability-remote-control-camera-access-mqtt">7,000 of them</a> from all around the world.</p> <p>The IoT is horribly insecure, but we <a href="https://www.schneier.com/books/click-here/">already knew that</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Uses 6 Flaws, 3 Zero-Days for Full Device Takeover
item.description: A new exploit kit for Apple iOS devices designed to steal sensitive data from is being wielded by multiple threat actors since at least November 2025, according to reports from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), iVerify, and Lookout. According to GTIG, multiple commercial surveillance vendors and suspected state-sponsored actors have utilized the full-chain exploit kit, codenamed DarkSword
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK: Regulation Drives Cyber Spending for Critical Infrastructure Orgs
item.description: 35% of security leaders working in the UK’s critical infrastructure said regulatory requirements are the primary influence on their security programs

item.title: EU Sanctions Companies in China, Iran for Cyberattacks
item.description: These rulings prohibit the entities from entering or doing business in the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: CISA Warns of Zimbra, SharePoint Flaw Exploits; Cisco Zero-Day Hit in Ransomware Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has urged government agencies to apply patches for two security flaws impacting Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) and Microsoft Office SharePoint, stating they have been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerabilities in question are as follows -
CVE-2025-66376 (CVSS score: 7.2) - A stored cross-site scripting
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: C2 Implant 'SnappyClient' Targets Crypto Wallets
item.description: In addition to enabling remote access, the malware supports a wide range of capabilities, including data theft and spying.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies &amp; Thieves Alike
item.description: A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: OFAC Sanctions DPRK IT Worker Network Funding WMD Programs Through Fake Remote Jobs
item.description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has sanctioned six individuals and two entities for their involvement in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) information technology (IT) worker scheme with an aim to defraud U.S. businesses and generate illicit revenue for the regime to fund its weapons of mass destruction (WMD) programs. "The North Korean
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple Rolls Out Real-Time Security Fixes Across iPhone, iPad, and Mac
item.description: <p>Apple launches Background Security Improvements to fix vulnerabilities in real time, starting with a WebKit flaw affecting Safari on iPhone, iPad, and Mac.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-background-security-improvements-webkit-fix/">Apple Rolls Out Real-Time Security Fixes Across iPhone, iPad, and Mac</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco FMC Zero-Day CVE-2026-20131 for Root Access
item.description: Amazon Threat Intelligence is warning of an active Interlock ransomware campaign that's exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-20131 (CVSS score: 10.0), a case of insecure deserialization of user-supplied Java byte stream, which could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
